summary: Bryan Cantrill announces Ian Murdock's hiring at Sun Microsystems, expressing enthusiasm about what this means for OpenSolaris. He praises Murdock's pragmatic approach to technology and his past support of OpenSolaris, particularly his defense against licensing FUD. Cantrill sees this as a major step toward combining the best of Debian's package management with Solaris's technical capabilities.
key_points: Ian Murdock, founder of Debian, has joined Sun Microsystems
Murdock previously defended OpenSolaris against licensing FUD from the Debian community
This hire represents progress toward combining Debian-style package management with OpenSolaris/DTrace
Cantrill hints at undisclosed work his team has been doing for the past year
money_quotes: I have always been impressed with Ian's decidedly pragmatic views of technology.
He took a decisive and courageous stand against some absurd Debian-borne anti-Nexenta licensing FUD.
Ian's arrival at Sun is a huge lurch forward towards the wide-spread productization of this ideal.
It looks like we're going to be able to apt-get our cake, and DTrace it too!
key_insight: Ian Murdock joining Sun represents a bridge between the Debian and OpenSolaris communities that could bring user-friendly package management to enterprise-grade systems software.
